mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth remains positive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146730 thousand as of August 2017 reflects continued labor market strength
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS growth decelerates below 50,000 persons for two consecutive months while SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of subsequent periods, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5 percent and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.5 percent year-over-year, the Fed is in a restrictive real-rate regime that constrains equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when both conditions are met simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.16% and CPIAUCSL at 245.03 as of August 2017
regime_hint: monetary normalization cycle with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of triggered instances over any 12-month period, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression against direction_5d.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2 percent month-over-month, signaling weakening consumer demand and future earnings compression. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when retail sales momentum falls below this critical threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 474841 million dollars as of August 2017
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 18-month rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03 when RSAFS is the sole predictor.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 2 percent year-over-year while unemployment remains stable below 4.5 percent, indicating supply-side capacity expansion without labor market stress. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when both conditions hold, reflecting sustained profit growth without wage inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.739 and UNRATE at 4.4% as of August 2017
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with productivity gains
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 24-month period, or the joint interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10 percent month-over-month, signaling a sharp contraction in residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1155 thousand units as of July 2017
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with housing market vulnerability
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over any 12-month rolling window, or the lagged correlation between HOUST declines and negative SPX returns is below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and forward earnings risk. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when unemployment momentum turns negative, as equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% as of August 2017, near cycle lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the predictive power of UNRATE deteriorates (Rank IC < 0.02) when unemployment is already elevated (> 5%).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2 percent annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 1.0 percent, indicating the Fed is tightening into slowing growth. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices recession risk and Fed policy error.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17030 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q2 2017, FEDFUNDS at 1.16%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with growth deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 24-month rolling window, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in logistic regression against direction_60d.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5 percent year-over-year, signaling stagflationary pressure and margin compression. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations contract under rising cost pressures and weakening output.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.739 and CPIAUCSL at 245.03 as of August 2017
regime_hint: any regime with simultaneous supply contraction and inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 70% of periods when both INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL accelerates, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
